properties.remark: THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN WOODEN FENCES IN KINGMAN ALONG WALNUT CREEK ROAD AND ALSO BLEW DOWN A 200 POUND SATELLITE DISH. IN ADDITION, THUNDERSTORM WINDS RIPPED THE ROOF OF A MOBILE HOME GARAGE. A 54MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED IN DOWNTOWN KINGMAN. THE TIME OF THESE EVENTS WERE ESTIMATED.
AN ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUST WAS REPORTED IN KINGMAN.
ROADS WERE REPORTED FLOODED IN THE KINGMAN AREA.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED DOWN A LARGE TREE IN LAKE HAVASU CITY. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN OFF OF SEVERAL BUSINESSES IN BULLHEAD CITY.
A SPOTTER MEASURED SUSTAINED WINDS OF 50 MPH WITH A GUST OF 64 MPH IN BULLHEAD CITY.

A SPOTTER ESTIMATED A 60 MPH WIND GUST IN BULLHEAD CITY IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY. THE WIND WAS STRONG ENOUGH TO KNOCK DOWN HIS NEIGHBOR WHO WAS OUTSIDE TRYING TO COLLECT HIS TRASH CANS.
BANK STREET WAS REPORTED FLOODED IN THE BUTLER AREA OF KINGMAN. THE MOUNT BULL ALERT GAGE RECORDED 1.16 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST 2 HOURS.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW AN AWNING AS WELL AS SOME SHINGLES OFF A HOUSE.

HEAVY RAINS CAUSED FLASH FLOODING AT THE CALICO GHOST TOWN AND WASHED OUT MOST OF THE CAMPGROUND. ROADS AT THE CAMGROUND WERE COVERED IN MUD AND DEBRIS. THE CALICO GHOST TOWN PARK LOST POWER DUE TO THE STORM. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
WATER...MUD AND ROCKS WERE CROSSING THE ROAD AT THE ENTRANCE TO THE PARK.
WEST CHINO DRIVE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING BETWEEN NORTH ESTRELLA ROAD AND MILKY WAY.
WATER...MUD...DEBRIS AND BOULDERS CROSSING U.S. 95 AT MILE MARKER 7.
A 300 YARD WIDE MUD FLOW OCCURRED JUST NORTH OF INDEPENDENCE. THERE HAVE BEEN 10 PEOPLE DISPLACED FROM THEIR HOMES. ALL ROADS SOUTH BOUND FROM THE RESERVATION ARE NOW BLOCKED WITH MUD.
ON STATE ROUTE 190 AT MILE MARKER 56 MUD AND ROCKS COVERING THE ROADWAY. THE HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED.
A PARK RANGER REPORTED ROCKS AND DEBRIS OVER DAYLIGHT PASS ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED WATER, MUD, AND DEBRIS CROSSING YERMO ROAD.
LOCAL STORM SPOTTER REPORTED 0.65 HUNDREDTHS OF AN INCH OF RAIN IN 45 MINUTES. WATER UP TO 1 FOOT CROSSING CITY STREETS AND COUNTY ROADS.

HEAVY THUNDERSTORM RAINS CAUSED FLASH FLOODING AT SOAPMINE ROAD AND STATE ROUTE 58. SEVERAL PARTIES WERE TRAPPED IN THEIR VEHICLES AND A SEARCH AND RESCUE HELICOPTER HAD TO BE CALLED. BY 955 PM PDT ALL PARTIES...5 PEOPLE...WERE RESCUED AND RUNOFF WAS SUBSIDING.
HIGHWAY 95 WAS WASHED OUT AT MILE MARKER 112
HIGHWAY 95 WAS WASHED OUT AT MILE MARKER 112.
ROADWAY FLOODING AT HWY 95 AND MM 43. HEAVY DEBRIS IN THE ROADWAY NEEDS TO BE CLEARED

AT MAIN STREET AND L STREET...FLOOD WATERS IN FIVE FOOT DEEP CULVERT TOPPING A SIX FOOT FENCE ABOVE THE CULVERT. THE FENCE WAS SWEPT AWAY ALONG WITH PORTIONS OF THE CONCRETE CULVERT. AT LEAST 3 HOMES FLOODED AT NEARBY H STREET.
LARGE AREAS OF STATE ROUTE 247 AT MILE MARKER 76 ARE FLOODED OUT WITH A LOT OF DEBRIS ON THE ROADWAY.
A PARK OFFICIAL RELAYED REPORTS FROM THE PUBLIC THAT HUALAPAI MOUNTAIN ROAD WAS FLOODED BY WATER RUNNING ACROSS IT FROM WASHES AND SEVERAL BOULDERS WERE ON THE ROAD AROUND MILE MARKER 11.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED MORNING STAR MINE ROAD IN THE MOJAVE NATIONAL PRESERVE WAS FLOODED.
NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED WATER RUNNING OVER ROADWAY AT THE INTERSECTION OF ROUTE 164 AND US 95.

NIPTON ROAD WAS REPORTED CLOSED BY THE NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL FROM THE CALIFORNIA-NEVADA STATE LINE TO THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 95 IN SEARCHLIGHT DUE TO WASHING OUT THE ROAD. THE ROAD IS CLOSED UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL HAS REPORTED FLOODING ACROSS HIGHWAY 62 AT THE INTERSECTIONS OF COPPER MOUNTAIN ROAD AND ROTARY DRIVE.
A SPOTTER HAS REPORTED FLOODING ACROSS ROUTE 62 AND CANYON ROAD.
A SPOTTER HAS REPORTED FLOODING AND FLOWING DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROADWAY AT THE INTERSECTION OF ROUTE 62 AND LEAR AVENUE.
SEVERAL APARTMENT UNITS WERE FLOODED AT THE SAGEWOOD APARTMENT COMPLEX IN TWENTYNINE PALMS. HOUSES IN THE HANSEN TRACT AREA WERE ALSO REPORTED FLOODED.
LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE REPORTED BROKEN OFF SEVERAL TREES IN LUCKIE PARK IN TWENTYNINE PALMS.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS DOWNED AT LEAST 3 POWER POLES IN TWENTYNINE PALMS. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED FLOODING AND STANDING WATER ACROSS THE ROADWAY ON ROUTE 62 AT MILE MARKER 42 AND 46.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THAT THERE A SWIFT WATER RESCUE OCCURRED AT THE MAIN GATE OF THE MARINE CORPS AIR STATION.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED NIPTON ROAD WAS CLOSED JUST EAST OF INTERSTATE 15 AND WATER WAS FLOWING OF THE ROAD.
ONE INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS WERE REPORTED DOWN IN THE GREEN VALLEY SECTION OF HENDERSON.
HIGHWAY 160 WAS CLOSED AT SPRING MOUNTAIN DUE TO MUD AND A BOULDER BEING WASHED IN THE ROAD BY FLOODWATERS.
HIGHWAY 160 IS CLOSED AT SPRING MOUNTAIN DUE TO BOULDER AND IN THE ROAD.
TWO FEET OF WATER AT SUNSET AND WHITNEY RANCH.
WATER IS CURB HEIGHT AND SPILLING INTO RIGHT LANE AT ARROYO GRANDE NEAR WIGWAM.
SEVERAL LARGE LEAF TREES WERE DOWNED IN HENDERSON.
HALF INCH OF RAIN IN TEN MINUTES FROM 325 TO 335.
FLOODWATERS CAUSED A VARIETY OF DAMAGE IN THE COMMUNITY OF MOUNTAIN SPRINGS. THE MOUNTAIN SPRINGS SALOON WAS FLOODED BY THREE FEET OF WATER. WATER ALSO FLOODED A HOUSE AND DAMAGED ASPHALT ON A ROAD.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE AT EAST TROPICANA AND US 95.
A 12 YEAR OLD BOY WAS RESCUED FROM A WASH IN HENDERSON NEAR PATRICK LANE AND STEPHANIE STREET.
BROADCAST MEDIA IN LAS VEGAS REPORTED THAT SEVERAL ROADS WERE FLOODED NEAR THE SAMS TOWN CASINO. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE AT US 95 AND EAST FLAMINGO.
WATER IS 10 INCHES DEEP AT HARMON AND BOULDER. CARS ARE STALLING.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE AT BOULDER HIGHWAY AND EAST TROPICANA.
THE NEEDLES AIRPORT MEASURED A 61 MPH WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
SPOTTER REPORTED 60 MPH WIND GUSTS AND 1.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN ABOUT 1.5 HOURS.
METRO SCANNER REPORTED WATER RUNING ON TORREY PINES DR SOUTH OF W DESERT INN ROAD...MAKING IT DIFFICULT TO DRIVE.
NELLIS AIR FORCE BASE MEASURED SUSTAINED WINDS OF 51 MPH FROM THE WEST AND A GUST OF 69 MPH IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
BROADCAST MEDIA IN LAS VEGAS REPORTED FLOODING ON ROADS IN THE WESTERN PART OF HENDERSON.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED EIGHT POWER POLES AS WELL AS 1 TO 4 INCH THICK TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN BY A THUNDERSTORM IN THE AREA.

THE QUAIL RUN SUBDIVISION OF MOHAVE VALLEY WAS FLOODED OUT FROM HEAVY RAINS. SEVERAL STREETS IN THE NEIGHBORHOOD WERE UNDER WATER AND RESIDENCE HOMES HAD TO BE SANDBAGGED TO PREVENT RISING WATERS FROM ENTERING THE HOUSES.
WASHES ALONG MOHAVE COUNTY RT 1 CAUSED MUD TO FLOW OVER THE ROAD.
*** 1 FATAL *** A SWIFT WATER RESCUE OCCURRED AT CAPISTRANO WAY AND RIO VISTA DRIVE. A VEHICLE GOT CAUGHT IN FLOOD WATERS AND TURNED UPSIDE DOWN. ONE PERSON WAS TRAPPED AND DIED.
THE CHEMEHUEVI WASH WAS REPORTED FLOODED ON US HIGHWAY 95 AT MILE MARKER 28. THE WATER ACROSS THE ROAD CAUSED HIGHWAY 95 TO BE CLOSED BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 28 AND 37.

THE MESQUITE CO-OP OBSERVER AND LOCAL MEDIA IN MESQUITE REPORTED THAT SEVERAL ROADS IN THE NORTHEAST PART OF THE CITY FLOODED DUE TO A THUNDERSTORM THAT PASSED OVER THE CITY. WATER WAS REPORTED TO BE AT LEAST 18 INCHES DEEP IN SOME INSTANCES. MUD AND ROCKS ALSO COVERED ROADS WERE FLOODING TOOK PLACE.

HUALAPAI PEAK ROAD BETWEEN MILE MARKER 10 AND 11 BECAME IMPASSIBLE DUE TO A FLASH FLOOD THAT CROSSED OVER THE ROADWAY. THE FLASH FLOOD WAS DUE TO RUNOFF FROM HUALAPAI PEAK...WHICH RECEIVED 1.85 INCHES OF RAIN.
SEVERAL ROADS IN LAKE HAVASU CITY WERE BLOCKED OR CLOSED DUE TO WATER RUNOFF OVER THE ROADWAYS FROM A THUNDERSTORM IN THE AREA.
THE MECURY MESOWEST SITE MEASURED A MAX WIND GUST AT 64 MPH WITH GUSTS ABOVE 60 MPH BETWEEN 315 PM AND 630 PM.
WINDS AT THE A14 MESONET SITE GUSTED TO 58 MPH BETWEEN 1230 PM AND 945 PM.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS ESTIMATED AT 50 MPH CAUSED DAMAGE TO A CARPORT AWNING AT A MOBILE HOME AND BLEW A FENCE DOWN NEXT TO A MOBILE HOME AS WELL AS CAUSING A CARPORT TO COLLAPSE ON ANOTHER MOBILE HOME. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
BROADCAST MEDIA IN LAS VEGAS REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF SAHARA AVENUE AND MARYLAND PARKWAY. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.

BROADCAST MEDIA IN LAS VEGAS REPORTED THAT HEAVY RAINS HAD CAUSED FLASH FLOODING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BOULDER HIGHWAY AND FLAMINGO ROAD. FLOWING WATER WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AT LEAST 8 INCHES DEEP ON THE ROADS AND WAS FLOWING OFF THE ROADS AND INTO PARKING LOTS. IN ADDITION, WATER WAS UP TO THE BOTTOM OF A BUS. FLOODWATERS OF AT LEAST A FOOT ALSO FORCED THE CLOSURE OF THE FLAMINGO ON-RAMP TO INTERSTATE 515. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.

HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA CREATED FLASH FLOODING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 127 SOUTH OF SHOSHONE. DEPUTY ESTIMATED 20 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE HIGHWAY IN AT LEAST TWO PLACES. VEHICLES WERE TRAPPED BETWEEN WATER CROSSING THE ROADWAY. DEPUTY LOST VEHICLE IN FLOOD WATER. NO INJURIES TO DEPUTY. AS OF 945 PM ROADWAY CLOSURES STILL IN EFFECT.
CROZIER CANYON NEAR TRUXTON ALERT STREAM GAGE REPORTED A CREST OF 5.22 FEET. IMPACTS UNKNOWN.
A USGS STREAM GAGE REPORTED A CREST OF 2.2 FEET DUE TO HEAVY RAIN IN THE AREA. IMPACTS UNKNOWN.
TRUXTON CANYON AT HIGHWAY 66 ALERT STREAM GAGE CRESTED AT 1.65 FEET. IMPACTS UNKNOWN.

HIGHWAY 95 HAD AN ESTIMATED 7 TO 10 INCHES OF WATER ON IT WITH MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS THE ROADWAY RESULTING IN A LANE CLOSURE. SOME RESIDENTIAL SIDE STREETS WERE ALSO CLOSED DUE TO WATER ON THEM.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THAT HIGH WINDS HAVE CAUSED TWO POWERPOLES TO BE BLOWN DOWN CAUSING POWER OUTAGES IN EAST KINGMAN. STRONG WINDS HAVE ALSO CAUSED A STORAGE SHED TO BE BLOWN OVER AND SEVERAL SWAMP COOLERS BLOWN OFF ROOFS.
